hero.description: Dr. Seffar Hicham is a specialist in gynecology-obstetrics. His career combines university hospital training in Morocco and a full year of clinical training at the ERASME University Hospital - Free University of Brussels (Belgium), with a constant commitment to international continuing education.

about.title: About Dr Seffar Hicham
about.subtitle: Your trusted gynecologist-obstetrician in Inezgane
about.pro_desc: Dr. Hicham Seffar places safety, diagnostic precision, modern minimally invasive techniques, and personalized human support at the heart of his practice. He is committed to offering high-quality care to patients in Inezgane and the region, inspired by international standards.

testimonials.subtitle: Here are the latest real testimonials left by our patients on our Google listing in Inezgane.

items.name: Anass belhajla
items.text: I highly recommend this clinic. The doctor really listens to his patients and doesn't rush the consultation; he explains everything in detail. Special mention for the reception staff who offer the best welcome.

items.name: Amina Bik
items.text: Dr. Hicham is one of the best doctors frankly, and even the girls working with him are very nice. Their handling is top, may God make it easy for them all.

items.name: Kaoutar Arabi
items.text: I want to testify to the great competence of Dr. Hicham Seffar. His professionalism, experience and quality of care reassured me enormously. I highly recommend him to all future mothers.

items.name: am fatima
items.text: Everything went well. I went with my sister this morning and we were very well received. The doctor is excellent: listens to the patient, exchanges, explains and shows great professionalism.

items.name: Bouchra Ghazi
items.text: If you are looking for a competent, professional, and above all human gynecologist to guide, advise and reassure you, you are at the right address and in good hands.

items.name: Yassine Benchabane
items.text: I highly recommend him, appointments are punctual, communication is very fluid and his knowledge and know-how are up to the mark... Machallah

footer.description: Your feminine health deserves high-level expertise. Dr. Hicham Seffar accompanies you with care in Inezgane.

articles.subtitle: Verified medical information, practical advice, and health news to accompany you at every stage of your life as a woman. Articles written by Dr. Hicham Seffar.
articles.summary_text: This guide provides you with reliable medical information and practical advice to take care of your health. For any specific question or concern regarding your personal situation, do not hesitate to consult Dr. Hicham Seffar for personalized support.

items.title: Vaginal Surgery: Hysterectomy, Prolapse and Aesthetics
items.excerpt: Vaginal surgery allows for the treatment of various uterine pathologies and the correction of prolapse without visible scarring, while offering perineal aesthetic solutions.
detailedContent: Vaginal Hysterectomy: A surgical technique allowing the removal of the uterus through natural routes, thus avoiding any abdominal scarring and promoting faster recovery.
Prolapse Repair: Surgical management of organ descent (bladder, uterus, rectum) to restore anatomy and improve patients' quality of life.
Perineal Aesthetic Surgery: Interventions aimed at correcting the sequelae of childbirth or improving the aesthetics of the intimate area (perineoplasty).
Advantages of the vaginal route: Less post-operative pain, reduced hospital stay, and absence of visible external scars.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Hysteroscopy: A precise exploration of the uterine cavity
items.excerpt: Discover how hysteroscopy allows for the diagnosis and treatment of uterine pathologies like polyps or fibroids with maximum precision and minimal post-operative consequences.
detailedContent: Precise diagnosis of intra-uterine anomalies (polyps, fibroids, septa) to treat abnormal bleeding or infertility. The examination allows for a direct and clear view of the entire uterine cavity.
Mini-invasive surgical treatment allowing the removal of lesions without any skin incision. The instrument passes through natural routes, avoiding visible scars.
Outpatient hysteroscopy: most procedures take place over half a day, allowing for a quick return home. Patient comfort is prioritized with modern anesthesia techniques.
Extremely fast post-operative recovery, with a return to usual activities the next day. Pain is generally minimal and well controlled by simple analgesics.
Personalized follow-up: each procedure is the subject of a detailed report and systematic histopathological analysis of samples if necessary for total medical safety.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Breast Surgery: Between prevention and treatment of mammary pathologies
items.excerpt: Surgical management of breast pathologies has evolved significantly. Discover the importance of precise diagnosis and advances in breast-conserving surgery.
detailedContent: Management of benign pathologies such as fibroadenomas, complex cysts, or atypical lesions. The goal is to treat the lesion while preserving the natural architecture of the breast as much as possible.
Diagnostic and treatment surgery for breast cancer: surgical biopsies and targeted excisions. Surgery remains a central pillar in the modern oncological care path.
Conservative surgery techniques (oncoplastic) aimed at removing the tumor while guaranteeing an optimal aesthetic result. Respect for body image is an absolute priority in our therapeutic approach.
Sentinel lymph node biopsy and analysis to limit functional sequelae in the arm. This precise technique often avoids complete axillary dissection and its potential complications.
Multidisciplinary support and rigorous post-operative follow-up for serene healing. Collaboration with radiologists, oncologists, and physiotherapists ensures comprehensive patient care.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Laparoscopy: Modern and mini-invasive gynecological surgery
items.excerpt: Less scarring, less pain, and faster recovery. Laparoscopy (coelioscopy) is revolutionizing the treatment of ovarian cysts and endometriosis.
detailedContent: Video-surgery technique allowing for exploration and operation of the abdomen via very small incisions (5 to 10 mm). The camera offers an enlarged and precise view of the pelvic organs.
First-line treatment for ovarian cysts, ectopic pregnancies, and certain forms of infertility. The precision of the surgical gesture allows for the preservation of future fertility.
Reference for the surgical treatment of endometriosis: precise removal of lesions while respecting surrounding healthy tissues. Laparoscopy significantly improves the patients' quality of life.
Major advantages: drastic reduction in post-operative pain and reduced risk of infection. Aesthetic damage is almost zero thanks to the small size of the scars.
Fast return to social and professional life: hospital stay is shortened and convalescence is much simpler than in classic 'open' surgery.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Importance of Prenatal Care: Complete guide for a serene pregnancy
items.excerpt: Regular prenatal follow-up is essential to ensure the health of mother and baby. Discover why every consultation counts and how to optimize your medical follow-up.
detailedContent: 7 essential prenatal consultations to monitor fetal development and prevent complications. These consultations allow for early detection of any anomaly and ensure personalized follow-up adapted to each pregnancy.

Screening ultrasounds in the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd trimesters to detect morphological anomalies or growth retardation. The first trimester ultrasound allows for precise dating of the pregnancy and screening for Down syndrome.
Systematic monitoring of blood pressure and weight gain to prevent preeclampsia. Gestational hypertension can have serious consequences and requires immediate management.

Screening for gestational diabetes between 24 and 28 weeks via glucose tolerance test. This screening is crucial because gestational diabetes increases risks of complications for both mother and child.

Preparation for breastfeeding and nutritional advice based on energy and micronutritional needs. A balanced diet rich in iron, calcium, folic acid, and omega-3 is essential for optimal fetal development.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Understanding the Menstrual Cycle: Signals and symptoms to recognize
items.excerpt: A complete guide to better understand your menstrual cycle and identify signs that require medical consultation. Learn to listen to your body.
detailedContent: Follicular phase (days 1-14): follicle development and estrogen production, preparation for ovulation. During this phase, the FSH hormone stimulates the growth of several ovarian follicles, one of which will become dominant.
Ovulatory phase: release of the oocyte, maximum fertility period. The LH peak triggers ovulation about 24-36 hours later. Cervical mucus becomes more fluid to facilitate sperm passage.
Luteal phase: progesterone secretion by the corpus luteum, preparation of the endometrium for implantation. This phase lasts about 14 days and ends with menstruation if no fertilization has occurred.
Warning signs: irregular cycles, intense pain that may signal hormonal disorders like polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) or endometriosis. Bleeding between periods can indicate a hormonal imbalance.
When to consult: amenorrhea for more than 3 months, heavy or prolonged abnormal bleeding, suspicion of endocrine disorder. Severe dysmenorrhea requires investigation to rule out endometriosis.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Menopause: practical guide for a harmonious transition
items.excerpt: Advice and information to better live through menopause and manage its symptoms daily with the help of your gynecologist. Natural solutions and treatments.
detailedContent: Hot flashes and night sweats: consequence of the drop in estrogen on the hypothalamic thermal center. These symptoms affect 75% of menopausal women and can last several years. Relaxation techniques and regular physical activity can help.

Hormone Replacement Therapy (HRT): hormonal balance to reduce symptoms and protect bone, to be evaluated according to cardiovascular history. HRT should be initiated within 10 years of menopause for maximum benefit and has absolute contraindications.

Osteoporosis prevention: sufficient intake of calcium (1200mg/day) and vitamin D (800 IU/day) to maintain bone density. A bone densitometry is recommended starting at age 65 or earlier in case of risk factors.

Adapted physical exercises: strength training and weight-bearing exercises to prevent bone loss. Fast walking, light jogging, and dancing are excellent. Regular physical activity also reduces cardiovascular risk.

Indispensable annual gynecological follow-up: screening for hormonal and oncological pathologies. Endometrial and ovarian cancers are more frequent after menopause. A cervical smear remains necessary until age 65.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Contraception: making the right choice according to your profile and needs
items.excerpt: Pill, IUD, implant... Discover the different contraceptive methods and find the one that suits you best according to your lifestyle and health.
detailedContent: Oral contraception: combined and progestogen-only, adjusted according to thromboembolic history. The combined pill is contraindicated in case of migraine with aura, smoking after 35, or history of venous thrombosis. Effectiveness depends on daily adherence.

Copper IUD: mechanical action on implantation and toxicity of copper on sperm, effective for up to 10 years. Without hormones, it suits women not wishing for hormonal contraception. May slightly increase menstrual flow.

Hormonal IUD: continuous release of levonorgestrel, duration 3 to 5 years, significant reduction in menstrual flow or even amenorrhea. Particularly indicated in case of menorrhagia or endometriosis. Very high satisfaction rate.

Contraceptive implant: continuous release of subcutaneous progestogen, discreet and effective for 3 years. Quick insertion under local anesthesia in the arm. May lead to irregular bleeding in the first months sometimes requiring additional treatment.

Annual consultation for control and adjustment according to tolerance and side effects. Clinical examination, blood pressure measurement, and cervical smear are part of the follow-up. Possibility to change methods at any time according to your evolving needs.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Preparation for Childbirth: Essential techniques and exercises
items.excerpt: How to well prepare physically and mentally for childbirth. Our advice to live this moment serenely with proven techniques.
detailedContent: Birth preparation sessions starting from the 6th month: breathing exercises and postures to facilitate labor. These group or individual sessions allow learning breathing techniques, relaxation, and positions favoring the baby's descent.
Breathing exercises and deep relaxation to reduce stress and pain. Deep abdominal breathing increases oxygenation and decreases pain perception. Sophrology and hypnosis can be effective supplements.

Epidural: loco-regional anesthesia technique by injection into the epidural space, administration timing according to cervical dilation generally starting from 3-4 cm. Allows staying conscious while suppressing the pain of contractions.

Recognizing the beginning of labor: regular contractions every 5 minutes for 2 hours, rupture of membranes with loss of clear amniotic fluid. Sometimes preceded by the loss of the mucus plug a few days before.

Maternity bag: checklist including comfortable clothes for the mother, bodysuits and pajamas for the newborn, hygiene products, medical documents (blood group card, medical file), and breastfeeding accessories if desired.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Fertility: advice and prevention to optimize your chances
items.excerpt: Factors that influence fertility and good practices to optimize your chances of conception. From diagnosis to natural solutions.
detailedContent: Fertility assessment after 12 months without pregnancy (6 months after age 35) to identify female and male causes. The assessment includes a spermogram for the man, hormonal dosages, and a pelvic ultrasound for the woman.

Hormonal analyses (FSH, LH, AMH, estradiol, progesterone) and pelvic ultrasound to evaluate ovarian reserve and detect possible cysts or fibroids. Anti-Müllerian Hormone (AMH) is an excellent marker of ovarian reserve.
Ovulation monitoring by basal body temperature curve, urinary hormonal ovulation tests, or ultrasound monitoring. The fertile period extends from 5 days before ovulation to 24 hours after.

Favorable factors: balanced diet rich in antioxidants, folic acid, and zinc, maintenance of a BMI between 19 and 25, regular moderate physical activity. Insufficient or excessive weight can disturb ovulation.

Avoid smoking (decreases ovarian reserve and oocyte quality), alcohol (toxic to gametes), and excessive stress (disturbs the hypothalamic-pituitary axis). Limit caffeine to 2 cups per day. Some endocrine disruptors can also affect fertility.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Breast Cancer Screening: Everything you need to know
items.excerpt: The importance of early breast cancer screening. Methods, frequency, and warning signs not to be neglected for your health.
detailedContent: Monthly self-palpation after periods to detect nodules, masses, or texture anomalies. Standing in front of a mirror, inspect your breasts by raising your arms, then palpate in circular movements lying down.
Annual clinical examination by a gynecologist or doctor for early detection of any anomaly. The examination includes visual inspection and palpation of the breasts and axillary lymph node areas.

Screening mammography every 2 years between 50 and 74 according to public health recommendations. This radiological examination allows for detection of non-palpable subclinical lesions. Can be started earlier in case of risk factors.
Complementary breast ultrasound if breast density is high in young women or anomalies detected on mammography. Breast MRI may be proposed in case of very high genetic risk.

Risk factors: first-degree family history, age (risk increases after 50), BRCA1/BRCA2 genetic mutations, late first pregnancy, lack of breastfeeding, prolonged hormone treatment. Genetic counseling is offered if multiple family histories exist.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Endometriosis: Diagnosis and modern management
items.excerpt: Understanding endometriosis, its symptoms, and new treatment approaches to improve patients' quality of life.
detailedContent: Characteristic symptoms: severe dysmenorrhea (very painful periods), chronic pelvic pain outside menstruation, deep dyspareunia (pain during intercourse), cyclic digestive disorders, possible infertility in 30-40% of patients.

Diagnosis: transvaginal ultrasound performed by an endometriosis expert and pelvic MRI to precisely visualize and locate endometriosis lesions (ovaries, uterosacral ligaments, rectovaginal septum). The diagnostic delay is unfortunately still long (7 years on average).

First-line medical treatments: non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs for pain, continuous hormonal contraception (continuous combined pill, levonorgestrel IUD), progestogens, GnRH agonists or antagonists inducing temporary artificial menopause.

Surgical treatment by laparoscopy if extensive lesions, large ovarian endometriomas, or severe symptoms resistant to medical treatment. Complete excision of lesions significantly improves fertility and quality of life.

Recommended multidisciplinary care: specialized gynecologist, pain specialist for chronic pain management, nutritionist (anti-inflammatory diet), specialized pelvic-perineal physiotherapist, psychologist for emotional support. Endometriosis significantly impacts quality of life.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham

items.title: Nutrition and Gynecological Health: Essential foods
items.excerpt: Discover how your diet influences your gynecological health and which foods to prioritize for optimal hormonal balance.
detailedContent: Omega-3 (fatty fish like salmon, sardines, mackerel, walnuts, flax seeds): powerful anti-inflammatory properties, reduction of menstrual pain, support of ovarian health, and improvement of oocyte quality. Recommended consumption: 2-3 portions of fatty fish per week.

Calcium (dairy products, sardines with bones, almonds, cabbage) and vitamin D (sun exposure, fatty fish, eggs): essential for maintaining bone density, preventing post-menopausal osteoporosis. Recommended intake: 1000-1200mg calcium/day and 800-1000 IU vitamin D/day.

Antioxidants (red fruits, citrus fruits, colorful vegetables, green tea): cellular protection against oxidative stress, hormonal regulation, improvement of fertility. Vitamins C and E protect oocytes. A diet rich in antioxidants also decreases the risk of certain gynecological cancers.
Dietary fibers (green vegetables, legumes, whole grains): promote the elimination of excess estrogen via stool, support sex hormone metabolism, stabilize blood sugar. Goal: 25-30g of fibers per day.

Adequate hydration: indispensable for all optimal metabolic and hormonal functions, toxin elimination, maintenance of vaginal lubrication. Drink 1.5 to 2 liters of water per day. Limit alcohol and caffeine which can disturb hormonal balance.
items.author: Dr. Seffar Hicham
